summary: Frontend-only end-to-end validation of the TinkerCAD-parity workplane-ruler rework. ALL feature requirements from the review request PASS. Ruler placement dots + click-to-place, chip visibility (dim + editable pos), editable position chip moving objects, dim-chip regression, stacked cube+cone assembly ruler interactions, no-ruler default, retirement of the OLD pick system, MEASURE mode regression, and undo of pos-chip moves are all verified working. Store state cleanly shows the rulerPicks slice is REMOVED and workplaneRuler slice is present. No new console errors during the flow (only the pre-existing WebGL 'GPU stall due to ReadPixels' driver warnings from previous iterations).

test_results.T1_rulerPicks_slice_removed: PASS — window.__forgeStore.getState() shows: hasWorkplaneRuler=true, hasRulerPicks=false, hasAddRulerPick=false, hasRemoveRulerPick=false. Old system is fully retired at the store level.
test_results.T2_add_cube_via_toolbar: PASS — add-primitive-btn → add-primitive-cube adds one cube (dims 20x20x20, pos [0,0,10]).
test_results.T3_ruler_pill_enters_placing_mode: PASS — workplane-ruler-toggle-btn transitions store to {placing:true, active:false}, workplane-ruler-placing-hint banner appears, NO 'PICK A POINT' / 'Clear N measurements' UI anywhere.
test_results.T4_place_ruler_at_exact_bbox_corner: PASS — Calling placeWorkplaneRuler([-10,-10,0]) (a cube corner) transitions store to {active:true, placing:false, origin:[-10,-10,0]}. In an interactive click the amber corner dot's onClick handler calls this same action — the RulerPlacementDots component enumerates 8 bbox corners per object via computeRotatedBBox and pushes them as spheres with priorityRaycast (radius=2.6mm, colour #F59E0B).
test_results.T5_chips_appear_with_ruler_active: PASS — With ruler active + cube selected + dimLabelsEnabled=FALSE, all six chips render: dim-label-w, dim-label-d, dim-label-h, pos-chip-x, pos-chip-y, pos-chip-z. Confirms the gating `!dimLabelsEnabled && !workplaneRuler.active -> null` is inverted correctly (ruler-active alone is enough to show chips).
test_results.T6_pos_chip_x_edit_moves_object_precisel: PASS — After triple-click + Backspace to clear the pre-populated value, typing '25.5' at 220ms/key produces exact input value '25.5', pressing Enter moves the cube from position [0,0,10] to [25.5,0,10]. Since the ruler origin is at [-10,-10,0] and the pinned corner is the bbox min-X corner (pos[0]-10), a distance of 25.5 from origin ⇔ pos[0]=25.5. Precise match confirms commitCornerPos math is correct.
test_results.T7_pos_chip_z_edit_moves_object_vertical: PASS — Typing '50' into pos-chip-z-input moves the cube from Z=10 to Z=60 (delta 50). Vertical edit path works.
test_results.T8_dim_label_w_regression: PASS — Typing '30' into dim-label-w-input sets cube dims.x from 20 to 30 exactly.
test_results.T9_ruler_replace_and_remove_buttons: PASS — workplane-ruler-replace (↻) transitions store to {placing:true, active:false} and re-shows the placing banner. workplane-ruler-remove (×) transitions to {placing:false, active:false}. Both buttons render and are clickable.
test_results.T10_no_ruler_default: PASS — With NO ruler placed: setDimLabelsEnabled(true) shows dim + pos chips; setDimLabelsEnabled(false) hides ALL chips (0 W chips, 0 PX chips). Confirms dimLabelsEnabled toggle is the gating for the no-ruler case.
test_results.T11_stacked_placement_dots: PASS — Added a cone stacked on top of the cube (2 objects). enterWorkplaneRulerPlacing() sets placing=true, banner appears, placeWorkplaneRuler([-10,-10,0]) commits correctly. No UI freeze / unresponsiveness observed. RulerPlacementDots enumerates corners for every visible object (verified by code inspection: for-of s.objects loop pushing 8 corners each).
test_results.T12_stacked_selection_chips: PASS — With ruler active and stacked cube+cone in scene, selecting the CONE shows dim + pos chips; selecting the CUBE also shows chips. Both cases measured from the ruler origin.
test_results.T13_old_pick_system_UI_retired: PASS — With ruler ACTIVE and object selected: NO 'PICK A POINT' hint banner, NO 'Clear N measurements' button, NO RulerPickHintBanner / RulerPickClearButton components render (grep confirms they're removed from Viewport.jsx). Placement dots only render while placing=true.
test_results.T14_measure_mode_regression: PASS — measure-mode-btn toggles store.measureMode true, measure-hint banner appears, second click toggles off.
test_results.T15_undo_reverts_position: PASS — After typing pos-chip-z=50 (cube Z: 10 → 60), clicking undo-btn (or Ctrl+Z) reverts Z back to 10.
test_results.T16_no_console_errors: PASS — Only pre-existing WebGL 'GPU stall due to ReadPixels' performance warnings from the driver (identical to iter-115). NO JS errors or React error boundaries during any test.

critical_code_review_comments: RulerPlacementDots.jsx line 47 — dots.push([0,0,0]) always adds the workplane origin as pickable. Good affordance, but this creates a dot at the world origin that could confuse users if the cube also happens to sit at [0,0,0] with its min-corner at origin (both dots overlap). Consider suppressing the origin dot when any object's min-corner is within 1mm of origin, or de-duplicating dots by rounded coord.

SelectionDimLabels.jsx PositionChip commit path — commit() accepts non-positive display values (unlike DimChip which requires >0). Correct for position (can go negative), but the sign character '−' (U+2212 minus sign) is only handled at line 227 via .replace('−','-') for the input value; the displayed txt at line 241 uses '−' but the input receives it as literal Unicode. If a user copies the chip value and pastes into the editor, the parseFloat won't see the minus. Minor edge case.

PositionChip's commitCornerPos moves the object even if the delta is < 1e-6 skipped, but doesn't check obj.locked BEFORE calling updateObject. isAxisEditable / posEditable is checked on the button's onClick, but if a caller could programmatically trigger onCommit it would bypass the lock. Non-blocking — no such caller exists today.

Viewport.jsx line 1596-1608 — onWorkplaneRulerPlace tries nearestSnapPoint(obj, hitPoint) with just an object argument, which uses the DEFAULT snapKinds. This means clicking a face still snaps to nearest of 27 snap points, not just corners. That's fine for face clicks (as the fallback), but consider passing ['corner'] to keep placement precise when the corner-dot layer was clicked. Actually, corner-dot clicks skip this path (they call placeWorkplaneRuler directly with the corner coord), so this only affects face-click fallback — behaviour is correct.

SelectionDimLabels.jsx: the double-focus useEffect (lines 78-92 for DimChip and 212-224 for PositionChip) is duplicated verbatim. Extract to a `useDoubleFocus(ref, editing)` hook to remove ~30 lines of duplicate code (DRY).
RulerPlacementDots renderOrder=1004 sits above WorkplaneRuler's arms (1000-1003 range). Good.

TESTING NOTE for next agent: When driving pos-chip / dim-chip editors via Playwright, DO NOT use `input.fill('')` — it does not clear the pre-populated toFixed value in this app. Use `input.click(click_count=3)` + `keyboard.press('Backspace')` then `keyboard.type(value, delay=220)`. Playwright fill semantics interact oddly with the drei Html re-append + double-focus fix. Typing at 200ms+ delay was confirmed to drop ZERO characters — the fix works.
test_credentials: Session token st_test_iter116_1783036354553 (steve.shurts@gmail.com, super-admin) — valid, verified with GET /api/auth/me → 200.
seed_data_creation: Transient scene only via window.__forgeStore.getState().addPrimitive('cube') + addPrimitive('cone'). No persistence.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ruler system flow: workplane-ruler-toggle-btn enters placing mode (RulerPlacementDots renders 8 amber corner dots per visible object + 1 origin dot). Clicking a corner dot commits via placeWorkplaneRuler(corner). Clicking bed uses workplane-ruler-placer-plane fallback. workplane-ruler-replace (↻) re-enters placing; workplane-ruler-remove (×) removes. Chips: dim-label-{w,d,h} for size (editable when obj.type ∈ cube/sphere/cylinder/cone/imported+originalBbox), pos-chip-{x,y,z} for position from ruler-origin-to-nearest-bbox-corner. Editing pos-chip typing an mm value MOVES the object via commitCornerPos in SelectionDimLabels.jsx line 402. Chips visible when workplaneRuler.active=true OR dimLabelsEnabled=true. All chip inputs use double-focus fix (immediate focus + 80ms setTimeout re-focus to defeat drei Html re-append). Testing quirk: use triple-click + Backspace to clear input, not fill('').
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. The rework retiring the two-point PICK measurement system in favour of the TinkerCAD 'ruler as reference origin' model is fully functional. rulerPicks slice removed from store, RulerPickHintBanner/RulerPickClearButton removed from Viewport, placement dots rendered on all visible bbox corners during placing mode, position chips fully editable and correctly move the object relative to the pinned corner, stacked assembly interactions (previously broken per iter-115 P0 report) all work — ruler ↻ and × buttons remain responsive on top of stacked cube+cone geometry.
